refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation traffic.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes close to vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Centralize teleoperation command processing in a dedicated Teleoperation module.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and alerting services within Safety Services.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Centralize teleoperation command processing in a dedicated Teleoperation module.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules for command and telemetry exchange.
Dependency on Network Link interface quality.
coupling_check.notes: DP3 logically isolates teleoperation processing, reducing diffusion and enabling focused latency optimization.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency.
Risk of latency spikes due to network congestion or processing overload.
Assumes Teleoperation module can be optimized independently.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit DP for latency monitoring (DP4) to enhance safety assurance.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing teleoperation commands, managing telemetry data, and ensuring low-latency communication.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send vehicle telemetry data to the Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from the operator.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send latency violation alerts to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of teleoperation logic and low diffusion; best architectural fit.
Contains vehicle hardware but teleoperation logic diffusion would be high.
Primarily for safety monitoring, not suitable for teleoperation processing.
Broad module, but lacks focus and ownership clarity for teleoperation latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation latency-critical processing in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and enables focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential interface latency overhead between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Dependency on network link quality outside module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and latency budgets for Network Link and Vehicle interfaces.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is measurable and aligns with safety criticality; DP selection logically addresses latency with clear separation.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by centralizing teleoperation logic in one module.
Interface latency overhead is a manageable risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with explicit interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit DP and IM elements for latency monitoring and alerting to enhance safety coverage.
Refine interface latency budgets and monitoring requirements.

decision.justification: Current level decomposition is complete, consistent, and meets safety criticality requirements with clear architectural boundaries and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

revise.why: To incorporate explicit latency monitoring and alerting design parameters for enhanced safety assurance.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can be optimized independently.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
